subject: current-affairs
description: Nobel Prizes & 2025 Winners MCQs for PPSC, FPSC, NTS, CSS & PMS.

mcqs.q: Who is the author of 'Gitanjali' and the first Asian, first Indian and first non-European to win the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913?
mcqs.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore won the 1913 Nobel Prize in Literature for 'Gitanjali' — the first Asian, Indian and non-European to do so.

mcqs.q: Who is the youngest person ever to win a Nobel Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Malala Yousafzai is the youngest Nobel laureate ever — Peace Prize 2014 at age 17.

mcqs.q: Who is the first female Pakistani to receive the Nobel Peace Prize (and the second Pakistani after Abdus Salam)?
mcqs.explanation: Malala Yousafzai is Pakistan's first female Nobel laureate (Peace 2014) and second Pakistani Nobel winner overall after Dr Abdus Salam (Physics 1979).

mcqs.q: Who won both the Nobel Prize in Literature and an Oscar Award?
mcqs.explanation: George Bernard Shaw won the 1925 Nobel Prize in Literature and a 1938 Academy Award for the screenplay of Pygmalion.

mcqs.q: In which year was the first Nobel Prize given in Economics?
mcqs.explanation: The Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences was first awarded in 1969.

mcqs.q: In how many fields is the Nobel Prize distributed?
mcqs.explanation: The Nobel Prize is awarded in six fields: Physics, Chemistry, Physiology or Medicine, Literature, Peace and Economic Sciences.

mcqs.q: In which discipline does the United States hold the record for the maximum number of Nobel Prize awards?
mcqs.explanation: The United States has won the most Nobel Prizes in the field of Medicine (Physiology or Medicine).

mcqs.q: Which country awards the Nobel Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Sweden awards the Nobel Prize (the Peace Prize is awarded in Oslo, Norway).

mcqs.q: What was the full name of Alfred Nobel?
mcqs.explanation: His full name was Alfred Bernhard Nobel.

mcqs.q: Marie Sklodowska-Curie received the Physics Prize in 1903 and the Chemistry Prize in:
mcqs.explanation: Marie Curie received her second Nobel Prize, in Chemistry, in 1911.

mcqs.q: Who won the first Nobel Peace Prize in 1901?
mcqs.explanation: The first Nobel Peace Prize (1901) was jointly awarded to Frédéric Passy and Henri Dunant.

mcqs.q: On which date is the Nobel Prize awarded each year?
mcqs.explanation: The Nobel Prize is awarded every year on 10 December — the death anniversary of Alfred Nobel.

mcqs.q: Which country has won the maximum Nobel Prizes in Literature to date?
mcqs.explanation: The USA holds the record for the most Nobel Prizes in Literature.

mcqs.q: Which country has won the most Nobel Prizes overall to date?
mcqs.explanation: The USA leads in total Nobel Prizes won across all categories.

mcqs.q: Which is the second largest Nobel Prize-winning country to date?
mcqs.explanation: The United Kingdom is second on the all-time Nobel laureate count.

mcqs.q: Which is the third largest Nobel Prize-winning country to date?
mcqs.explanation: Germany is third on the all-time Nobel laureate count.

mcqs.q: In which year did the maximum number of women win a Nobel Prize?
mcqs.explanation: In 2009, a record number of women laureates were awarded Nobel Prizes.

mcqs.q: In which category has the USA won the maximum number of Nobel Prizes?
mcqs.explanation: The USA's largest tally of Nobel Prizes is in Physics.

mcqs.q: Which woman was awarded two Nobel Prizes for two different sciences?
mcqs.explanation: Marie Curie won the 1903 Physics Prize and the 1911 Chemistry Prize.

mcqs.q: Who is the first woman winner of the Nobel Prize (Physics 1903)?
mcqs.explanation: Marie Curie became the first woman Nobel laureate when she shared the 1903 Physics Prize.

mcqs.q: The 1979 Nobel Peace Prize winner whose real name was Agnes Gonxha Bojaxhiu was universally known as:
mcqs.explanation: Mother Teresa (born Agnes Gonxha Bojaxhiu, ethnically Albanian) won the 1979 Nobel Peace Prize.

mcqs.q: Who won the first Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1901?
mcqs.explanation: Jacobus Henricus van 't Hoff won the first Nobel Prize in Chemistry (1901).

mcqs.q: Who is the only person to win two unshared Nobel Prizes?
mcqs.explanation: Linus Pauling is the only person ever to receive two UNSHARED Nobel Prizes — Chemistry 1954 and Peace 1962.

mcqs.q: Which country awards the Nobel Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Sweden awards the Nobel Prize (Peace Prize at Oslo, Norway).

mcqs.q: Who won the first Nobel Prize in Physics in 1901?
mcqs.explanation: Wilhelm Conrad Röntgen won the first Nobel Prize in Physics (1901) for the discovery of X-rays.

mcqs.q: This famous US chemist won a Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 1954 and the Nobel Peace Prize in 1962 for opposition to nuclear testing. Who was he?
mcqs.explanation: Linus Pauling — the only person to win two unshared Nobels — was a vocal opponent of atmospheric nuclear testing.

mcqs.q: Alfred Nobel was the inventor of which high explosive?
mcqs.explanation: Alfred Nobel invented dynamite (patented 1867) and used the wealth from his patents to fund the Nobel Prize.

mcqs.q: What was the name of the Burmese (Myanmar) woman who won the 1991 Nobel Peace Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Aung San Suu Kyi won the Nobel Peace Prize in 1991 for her non-violent struggle for democracy in Myanmar.

mcqs.q: Which Nobel Prize is also awarded to organisations, besides individuals?
mcqs.explanation: Only the Nobel Peace Prize can be awarded to organisations (e.g. ICRC, UN, OPCW, EU).

mcqs.q: Alfred Bernhard Nobel — who instituted the Nobel Prize — belonged to which country?
mcqs.explanation: Alfred Nobel was a Swedish chemist and industrialist.

mcqs.q: Which Nobel Prize has been awarded continuously from its institution date?
mcqs.explanation: The Nobel Memorial Prize in Economic Sciences (instituted 1968, first awarded 1969) has been awarded every year since its institution.

mcqs.q: Who jointly won the 1979 Nobel Prize in Physics?
mcqs.explanation: The 1979 Physics Prize was shared by Steven Weinberg, Abdus Salam and Sheldon Glashow for the electroweak unification theory.

mcqs.q: Who received the Nobel Prize twice in Chemistry — in 1958 and 1980?
mcqs.explanation: British biochemist Frederick Sanger won the Chemistry Prize twice (1958 and 1980).

mcqs.q: Who received the Nobel Prize twice in Physics — in 1956 and 1972?
mcqs.explanation: John Bardeen is the only person to have won two Nobel Prizes in Physics (1956 and 1972).

mcqs.q: Who won the 1954 Chemistry Prize and the 1962 Peace Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Linus Pauling won the 1954 Chemistry Prize and the 1962 Peace Prize.

mcqs.q: Tawakkol Karman, the second Muslim woman after Shirin Ebadi to win the Nobel Peace Prize (2011), is from:
mcqs.explanation: Tawakkol Karman of Yemen won the 2011 Nobel Peace Prize.

mcqs.q: After Malala Yousafzai received the Peace Nobel Prize in 2014, which records were established?
options: The youngest person ever to win a Nobel Prize
Second Pakistani to win a Nobel Prize
First female Pakistani to win a Nobel Peace Prize
mcqs.explanation: Malala became the youngest Nobel laureate, the second Pakistani after Abdus Salam, and the first female Pakistani Nobel laureate.

mcqs.q: Name the first international organisation to win the Nobel Prize.
options: International Committee of the Red Cross
mcqs.explanation: The Institute of International Law was the first international organisation to win the Nobel Peace Prize (1904).

mcqs.q: How many US Presidents have won the Nobel Peace Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Four US Presidents have won the Nobel Peace Prize: Theodore Roosevelt (1906), Woodrow Wilson (1919), Jimmy Carter (2002) and Barack Obama (2009).

mcqs.q: Who is the first Muslim woman to receive the Nobel Peace Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Iranian human-rights lawyer Shirin Ebadi (2003) was the first Muslim woman to win the Nobel Peace Prize.

mcqs.q: Who is the first Muslim ever to receive the Nobel Peace Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Egyptian President Anwar El Sadat won the 1978 Nobel Peace Prize — the first Muslim recipient.

mcqs.q: Dr Abdus Salam — the first Pakistani Nobel laureate (1979) — won in:
mcqs.explanation: Dr Abdus Salam won the 1979 Nobel Prize in Physics for the electroweak unification theory.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Nobel Foundation instituted?
mcqs.explanation: The Nobel Foundation was instituted in 1900 (first prizes awarded in 1901).

mcqs.q: The Peace Nobel Prize is awarded annually at:
mcqs.explanation: The Nobel Peace Prize is awarded in Oslo, Norway. The other five Nobel Prizes are awarded in Stockholm.

mcqs.q: In which year was the Nobel Prize given for the first time?
mcqs.explanation: The first Nobel Prizes were awarded in 1901.

mcqs.q: Which country won the maximum Nobel Prizes in 2016?
mcqs.explanation: As per this MCQ source, the UK won 5 Nobel Prizes in 2016, the most of any country that year.

mcqs.q: How many people have won more than one Nobel Prize?
mcqs.explanation: Five people have won more than one Nobel Prize — Marie Curie, Linus Pauling, John Bardeen, Frederick Sanger and Karl Barry Sharpless (plus organisations like ICRC and UNHCR).

mcqs.q: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Chemistry was awarded jointly to Susumu Kitagawa, Richard Robson and Omar M. Yaghi for the development of:
mcqs.explanation: The 2025 Chemistry Nobel was awarded for the development of metal-organic frameworks (MOFs).

mcqs.q: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences was awarded to Joel Mokyr, Peter Howitt and Philippe Aghion for their work on:
options: Sustained growth through technological progress and the theory of creative destruction
mcqs.explanation: The 2025 Economics Nobel honoured the trio's work on prerequisites for sustained growth via technological progress and 'creative destruction'.

mcqs.q: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Literature was awarded to:
mcqs.explanation: Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai won the 2025 Nobel Prize in Literature.

mcqs.q: László Krasznahorkai — the 2025 Nobel laureate in Literature — is from which country?
mcqs.explanation: László Krasznahorkai is a Hungarian novelist.

mcqs.q: The 2025 Nobel Peace Prize was awarded to:
mcqs.explanation: María Corina Machado of Venezuela won the 2025 Nobel Peace Prize.

mcqs.q: María Corina Machado — the 2025 Nobel Peace Prize laureate — is a democracy advocate from which country?
mcqs.explanation: María Corina Machado is a Venezuelan opposition leader and democracy advocate.

mcqs.q: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Physics was awarded jointly to John Clarke, Michel H. Devoret and John M. Martinis for the discovery of:
options: Macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electric circuit
mcqs.explanation: The 2025 Physics Nobel honoured macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electric circuit.

mcqs.q: The 2025 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine was awarded jointly to Mary E. Brunkow, Fred Ramsdell and Shimon Sakaguchi for their discoveries concerning:
mcqs.explanation: The 2025 Medicine Nobel was awarded for discoveries on peripheral immune tolerance — how the immune system avoids attacking the body itself.

mcqs.q: On what date are the Nobel Prizes formally awarded each year?
mcqs.explanation: All Nobel Prizes are formally awarded on 10 December — the death anniversary of Alfred Nobel.

mcqs.q: In what month are Nobel Prize winners typically announced?
mcqs.explanation: The Nobel Prize laureates are announced each year in October.

mcqs.q: Where is the Nobel Peace Prize awarded?
mcqs.explanation: The Nobel Peace Prize is awarded in Oslo, Norway. The other five prizes are awarded in Stockholm, Sweden.

mcqs.q: How many Nobel Prize winners were honoured in the 2025 Chemistry, Physics and Medicine categories combined?
mcqs.explanation: Three winners each in Chemistry, Physics and Medicine = 9 individuals were honoured across these three sciences in 2025.
